name: Miso Chocolate Tuile with Caramelized Cocoa Nibs
description: A delicate, lacy chocolate tuile infused with umami-rich miso, paired with crunchy caramelized cocoa nibs — an interplay of sweet, salty, and bitter elements.
keywords: miso, chocolate, tuile, cocoa nibs, caramel, dessert, modernist, pastry

winePairings.name: Madeira Sercial
winePairings.notes: Nutty, salty-sweet profile harmonizes with miso/chocolate.

winePairings.name: Madeira Malmsey 20-year
winePairings.notes: Dense caramel and spice enhance deep cocoa notes.

steps.instruction: Plate the tuile alongside a quenelle (smooth, oval-shaped scoop) of chocolate ganache.
Scatter caramelized cocoa nibs over or around for texture and contrast.
Serve immediately for maximum crispness and aromatic impact.
Preheat oven to 170°C (340°F) and line a baking tray with a silicone mat.
Whisk together melted butter, powdered sugar, miso paste, and egg white until smooth.
Sift in flour, cocoa powder, and a pinch of salt; mix until combined.
Spread thinly onto silicone mat using an offset spatula into desired shapes (rounds or ovals).
Bake for 7–8 minutes until edges darken slightly and tuile is lacy.
While hot, drape over a rolling pin or mold for a curved shape if desired. Cool until crisp.
Combine sugar and water in a small pan and heat until sugar dissolves.
Add cocoa nibs and stir continuously until sugar caramelizes and coats nibs evenly.
Remove from heat, spread on parchment paper, and sprinkle with a pinch of sea salt.
Cool completely and break apart any clusters.
Heat cream until just simmering, pour over chocolate, stir until smooth.
